name: Diablo
description: Spicier content pool. Same mechanics, escalated intent. Adults only.

forfeits.text: {{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} something personal — dating, body count, last hook-up, anything they'd never bring up sober. Answer → {{biasedRandom}} takes 2. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 2.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a question designed to make them squirm. Honest answer → {{biasedRandom}} takes 3. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 3.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} something they have never admitted to this group. Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 4. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 4.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} about an ex, a kink, or a fantasy. Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 5. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 5.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} what they'd actually do if no one in this room would find out. Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 6. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 6.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} about the most recent person in this group they've thought about in a non-platonic way. Answer → {{biasedRandom}} takes 7. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 7.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} a question designed to cross a line — sexual, secretive, or both. Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 8. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 8.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} to rank the group by any criterion that will cause drama. {{drawer}} answers honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 9. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 9.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} for a confession the group doesn't know. Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 10.

True or false — x-rated edition. {{drawer}} makes a sexual statement about themselves; group guesses. Distribute 10 among the wrong-guessers. All correct → {{drawer}} takes 10. {{drawer}} refuses to play → {{drawer}} takes 10.

{{drawer}} reveals an unambiguously sexual secret about themselves. News to everyone → {{drawer}} distributes 10. Anyone already knew → {{drawer}} takes 10. {{drawer}} refuses to reveal → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{biasedRandom}} asks {{drawer}} for something they've fantasised about but never done. Answer honestly → {{biasedRandom}} takes 15. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 15.
Ace of Secretos. The three loudest players (highest raw penalties) take 15 / 10 / 5. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to do something uncomfortable. Do it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 2. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 2.
{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to break a social norm right now. Do it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 3. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 3.
{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to do something they'd normally be too embarrassed to do sober. Do it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 4. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 4.
{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to do something mildly scandalous. Do it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 5. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 5.
{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to do something that will make the group cringe. Do it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 6. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 6.
{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to reveal, send, or show something. Do it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 7. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 7.
{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to do something that crosses one of their lines. Do it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 8. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 8.
{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to do something suggestive in front of the group. Do it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 9. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 9.
{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to do something they'll remember in the morning. Do it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{drawer}} dares {{biasedRandom}} to do something bold. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Refuse → {{drawer}} takes 10.
{{drawer}} is invited to send a flirtatious / suggestive message to {{biasedRandom}}. Send it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 10. Refuse → {{drawer}} takes 10.

{{biasedRandom}} dares {{drawer}} to do something significantly more daring — the kind of dare you'd only accept after a few drinks. Complete it → {{biasedRandom}} takes 15. Cop-out → {{drawer}} takes 15.
Ace of Riesgos. The player with the lowest penalty count takes half the gap to the highest. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks a spicy superlative. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 2.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks a superlative designed to cause friction. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 3.

'Who is the most [X]?' — judgmental edition. {{drawer}} picks, group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 4.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks a sexual or dating-related superlative. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 5.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks something pointed. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 6.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks a ranking that'll upset someone. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 7.
'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks, group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 8.

'Who is the most [X]?' — honesty hurts edition. {{drawer}} picks, group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 9.

'Who is the most [X]?' — {{drawer}} picks the most damning superlative they can think of. Group votes. Split: {{drawer}} takes the bigger half, the most-voted takes the rest. {{drawer}} refuses to pick → {{drawer}} takes all 10.
{{drawer}} names a spicier shared dare for themselves and {{biasedRandom}}. Both complete it → distribute 10 among the rest of the group. Either refuses → refuser takes 10. Both refuse → 5 each.
{{biasedRandom}} is invited to share a kiss with {{drawer}}. Both in → distribute 10 among everyone else. One declines → they take 10. Both decline → 5 each.

{{drawer}} names a significantly harder shared dare for themselves and {{biasedRandom}} — the kind you'd only accept after a few drinks. Both complete it → distribute 15 among the rest of the group. Either refuses → refuser takes 15. Both refuse → {{drawer}} takes 8, {{biasedRandom}} takes 7.
Ace of Sorbo. The three soberest players (lowest raw penalties) take 15 / 10 / 5. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
Mini-game: Nose. {{drawer}} calls 'NOSE'. Last to touch their index finger to their nose loses 2. Refuse to play → take 2.
Mini-game: Three-word. Starting with {{drawer}}, build a sentence one word at a time. First to break (or stall) loses 3. Refuse to play → take 3.
Mini-game: Categories. {{drawer}} picks a category (keep it spicy), then names an item. Going around, each player names another. Repeat or blank → lose 4. Refuse to play → take 4.
Mini-game: Thumb War. {{drawer}} picks any challenger. Loser takes 5. Refuse to play → take 5.
Mini-game: Countdown. {{drawer}} counts silently in their head. Everyone shouts 'STOP' at the moment they think is 10 seconds. Furthest from 10 loses 6. Refuse to play → take 6.
Mini-game: Rhyme Chain. {{drawer}} says a word. Going around, each player rhymes. Break the chain → lose 7. Refuse to play → take 7.
Mini-game: Staring. {{drawer}} picks any challenger. First to blink, laugh, or look away loses 8. Refuse to play → take 8.

Mini-game: Odds (Diablo). {{drawer}} challenges any player 'what are the odds you'd X?' (X is spicier than usual). Pick a ceiling, 3-2-1, both say a number. Match → challenged does it or cops out (takes 9). No match → {{drawer}} takes 9.
Mini-game: Most-Likely. {{drawer}} poses a 'who's most likely to X?' question (go wild). Group votes. Most-voted takes 10. Refuse to play → {{drawer}} takes 10.
Drawer's Challenge (Diablo). {{drawer}} invents a spicier challenge and picks any player to attempt it. Complete → {{drawer}} takes 10. Fail or refuse → target takes 10.
1v1 Rock-Paper-Scissors. {{drawer}} picks any player. One round. Loser takes 10. Either refuses → refuser takes 10.
Picante Roulette. On '3', everyone (including {{drawer}}) simultaneously points at one other player. Most-pointed-at takes 15. Tie → {{drawer}} chooses. Anyone refuses to point → they take 15.
Ace of Locura. The loudest player (highest raw penalties) takes half the gap to the soberest. {{drawer}} takes an extra 5.
